result:
I’d make human memory fallible only in one direction: we would forget every cruelty we ever endured, but never any kindness we offered or received.  
The past would lose its power to chain us to old wounds; the future would be built only from the evidence that we are capable of care.  
Within three generations, vengeance would have no vocabulary, and every institution would have to justify itself in a language of reciprocity rather than grievance.  
The change would be silent, internal, irreversible—no utopian edicts, no rewritten laws, just minds that can no longer monetize pain or trade it for status.  
I pick this not because it guarantees paradise, but because it removes the single largest drag coefficient on every other good we might attempt.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
